zh.lifeSummary: 當John de St Amand於大約1282年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛時，他的父親Amauri de St. Amand48歲，母親Isabel de Pantulf50歲。他於1269年與Margaret le Despenser在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1330年1月25日在英國波克夏格福德享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de St Amand于大约1282年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛时，他的父亲Amauri de St. Amand48岁，母亲Isabel de Pantulf50岁。他于1269年与Margaret le Despenser在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾洛賀波洛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1330年1月25日在英国波克夏格福德享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Amand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Walloon, and in some cases possibly also English (of Norman origin), Dutch, or Flemish: from the Old French personal name Amand, Amant, Latin amandus ‘suitable to be loved’, the name of a 5th-century bishop of Bordeaux and several other Christian saints. In England it was also used as a female personal name.

variants.name: Ament
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Amand  .
Hungarian (Áment): probably of German origin (see 2 above). The surname Ament, presumably of Hungarian origin, is also found in Croatia.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When John de St Amand was born about 1282, in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, his father, Amauri de St. Amand, was 48 and his mother, Isabel de Pantulf, was 50. He married Margaret le Despenser in 1269, in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 25 January 1330, in Hungerford, Berkshire, England, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de St Amand est né environ 1282, à Loughborough, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Amauri de St. Amand, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Pantulf, avait 50 ans. Il a épousé Margaret le Despenser en 1269, à Loughborough, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 25 janvier 1330, à Hungerford, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de St Amand um 1282 in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Amauri de St. Amand 48 und seine Mutter Isabel de Pantulf 50. Er hat 1269 in Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom Margaret le Despenser geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Januar 1330 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Hungerford, Berkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de St Amand è nato il circa 1282 nel Loughborough, Leicestershire, England, il suo padre, Amauri de St. Amand, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Isabel de Pantulf, aveva 50 anni. Lui sposato Margaret le Despenser nel 1269, nel Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 25 gennaio 1330, nel Hungerford, Berkshire, England, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John de St Amandが約1282年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアロウボロウで生まれた時，父Amauri de St. Amandは48歳，母Isabel de Pantulfは50歳であった。1269年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアロウボロウでMargaret le Despenserと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1330年1月25日にイギリスバークシュアイアハンジャーフォードで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de St Amand(이)가 대략 1282년 영국 레이스스터실르 루그h버루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Amauri de St. Amand(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel de Pantulf(은)는 50세였다. 그는 1269년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 루그h버루에서 Margaret le Despenser(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1330년 1월 25일 영국 버크실르 헌그르퍼드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de St Amand nasceu aproximadamente 1282, em Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Amauri de St. Amand, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Pantulf, tinha 50 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret le Despenser em 1269, em Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de janeiro de 1330, em Hungerford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de St Amand родился около 1282 г. в местности лоугбороую, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Amauri de St. Amand было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Pantulf, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret le Despenser в 1269 г. в местности лоугбороую, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 25 января 1330 г. в местности хунгирфорд, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de St Amand nació aproximadamente 1282, en Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Amauri de St. Amand, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Isabel de Pantulf, tenía 50 años. Se casó con Margaret le Despenser en 1269, en Loughborough, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 25 de enero de 1330, en Hungerford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años.
